description_long: This is a roughly hewn, trapezoidal stone block with a flat top surface and intricately carved decorative motifs on three of its sides. The carvings feature interwoven vine or scroll patterns, suggesting a historical or ornamental function. The stone's surface is weathered, with visible chips and irregularities, especially along the edges and corners. The back side is unadorned and more roughly finished, indicating it may have been positioned against another structure or out of sight. The coloration is a consistent light beige to pale gray, typical of limestone or sandstone. Its substantial mass and solid construction make it suitable as a barrier or architectural element in an urban or historical setting.
description: A heavy, trapezoidal stone block with ornate vine-like carvings on three sides and a flat, weathered top surface. The back is rough and undecorated, suggesting it was meant to be partially embedded or placed against another structure.
description_view_0: Front view shows a flat top and a face with prominent scrollwork carvings, with the block tapering downward.
description_view_1: Side view reveals a different carved pattern with more pronounced vine motifs and a slightly slanted top edge.
description_view_2: Rear view displays a rough, undecorated surface with visible chipping and no carvings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view shows another carved face, less detailed than the front but still featuring vine-like reliefs.
attribute_location_context: historic urban plaza or architectural facade
attribute_installation_method: dry-stacked or mortared into a wall or barrier
attribute_weathering_pattern: Edges and corners are chipped and worn, with a generally rough, aged surface indicating prolonged exposure to the elements.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: Visible decorative carvings with vine and scroll motifs on three sides.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: Likely forms part of a continuous wall or decorative barrier.
